etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "tree") with an added stroke at the top -> indicating the top or end -> final form 末(mò, "end")
etymology.interpretation: The character 末(mò, "end") represents a tree 木(木) (mù, "tree") with an additional stroke at the top of its trunk. This design suggests the concept of the 'end' or 'apex' as it visually distinguishes the topmost part of the tree, highlighting where it finishes. It emphasizes the concept of reaching the maximum point or the termination of something.

examples.chinese: 这个故事的结尾很感人(這個故事的結尾很感人)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège gùshì de jiéwěi hěn gǎnrén.
examples.english: The ending of this story is very touching.

examples.chinese: 年底时(年底時)，大家都很忙(大家都很忙)。
examples.english: At the end of the year, everyone is very busy.

examples.chinese: 电影的末尾有一个意想不到的转折(電影的末尾有一個意想不到的轉折)。
examples.pinyin: Diànyǐng de mòwěi yǒu yīgè yìxiǎngbùdào de zhuǎnzhé.
examples.english: There is an unexpected twist at the end of the movie.
